info.title: EdgeHub API
info.description: EdgeHub Control Plane API — secure, asynchronous REST endpoints for edge node provisioning, telemetry ingestion, and fleet management across heterogeneous environments (bare-metal, Docker, Kubernetes).

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Admin authentication. Use your Admin API Key to obtain a session cookie required for all control-plane operations.

tags.name: sites
tags.description: Sites are the top-level organizational unit (e.g. a datacenter, a remote location, a logical group). Each site owns its nodes and registration tokens, and can have up to two Webhook integrations (Discord, Slack).

tags.name: tokens
tags.description: Single-use registration tokens scoped to a specific site. The agent presents this token once to register itself; the backend burns it immediately upon use to prevent replay attacks.

tags.name: nodes
tags.description: Edge nodes registered under a site. Exposes full CRUD, telemetry history, and JWT renewal operations.

tags.name: agents
tags.description: Agent-facing endpoints. These are called by the EdgeHub Go agent, not by the dashboard. Registration is token-authenticated; all subsequent calls use a Bearer JWT.

post.description: Authenticates an administrator using an Admin API Key. On success, the server sets a secure session cookie that must be included in all subsequent control-plane requests. The cookie is HttpOnly and scoped to the API origin.

200.description: Login successful. Session cookie is set in the response headers.

post.description: Invalidates the current admin session by clearing the session cookie. Subsequent requests using the old cookie will be rejected with 401.

get.description: Returns all sites belonging to the authenticated admin. Each site includes its Webhook configuration (URLs are returned if set).

post.description: Creates a new logical site. Optionally configure Discord and/or Slack Webhook URLs to receive alerting notifications when nodes go offline or recover. Webhook URLs can be added or changed later via `PATCH /api/v1/sites/{site_id}`.

get.description: Retrieves the full details of a single site by its UUID, including Webhook URLs and creation timestamp.

patch.description: Partially updates a site's mutable fields. All fields are optional — only the fields provided in the request body will be updated. To remove a Webhook URL, pass `null` for that field.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: UUID of the site to update.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a site. **Cascade behavior:** all nodes, registration tokens, and heartbeat records belonging to this site are also deleted. This action is irreversible.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: UUID of the site to delete.

204.description: Site deleted successfully. No content returned.

get.description: Returns all nodes registered under a specific site, including their latest telemetry snapshot (CPU, RAM, disk, uptime), current status, and offline counter. Equivalent to `GET /api/v1/nodes/` filtered by site.

200.description: Array of nodes belonging to the site.

post.description:
Generates a new single-use registration token scoped to this site. The raw token value is returned **only once** in the response — it is not stored in plaintext and cannot be retrieved again. Copy it immediately and use it to deploy the EdgeHub agent.

Tokens are valid for a configurable duration (`expires_in_hours`, default 24h, max 8760h). Once used by an agent, the token is permanently burned and cannot be reused.

parameters.name: site_id
parameters.description: UUID of the site this token grants registration access to.

201.description: Token created. The `raw_token` field contains the plaintext value — save it now, it will not be shown again. The `commands` field contains pre-built copy-paste deployment commands for all agent modes.

get.description: Returns all registration tokens for a site. For security, the `raw_token` plaintext is **not** included in list responses — only metadata (ID, label, expiry, validity status) is returned.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a registration token, preventing it from being used for future agent registrations. If the token was already consumed by an agent, this operation only removes the token record — the registered node is unaffected.

parameters.name: token_id
parameters.description: UUID of the token to revoke.

post.description:
**Called by the EdgeHub agent, not the dashboard.**

Registers a new edge node using a single-use provisioning token. The flow is:
1. Agent presents `registration_token` along with its hardware metadata.
2. Backend validates the token, **immediately burns it** (single-use), and creates a new node record.
3. Backend returns the `node_id` and a long-lived `agent_token` (JWT).
4. The agent stores this JWT in `edgehub-state.json` on disk and uses it for all future heartbeats.

Subsequent agent restarts skip this step entirely — the agent reads the JWT from the state file and proceeds directly to the heartbeat loop.

201.description: Node registered successfully. The `agent_token` is the JWT the agent must use for all subsequent Bearer-authenticated requests. Store it securely — it will not be re-issued without an explicit JWT renewal.

401.description: Invalid, expired, or already-consumed registration token.

post.description:
**Called by the EdgeHub agent on every telemetry cycle.**

The agent sends a full snapshot of current hardware metrics. The backend:
- Updates the node's `last_seen` timestamp and resets the `offline_cycles` counter.
- Persists the telemetry snapshot to the heartbeat history.
- Returns any pending `command` for the agent to execute (e.g. `update_jwt` for JWT rotation).

**Offline detection:** if the backend stops receiving heartbeats, it increments `offline_cycles` on each check interval. After 3 consecutive missed cycles, the node is marked offline and Webhook alerts are dispatched.

**Revocation:** if this node has been deleted from the dashboard, the backend returns `401 Unauthorized`. The agent treats this as a permanent termination signal — it deletes its local state file and halts.

200.description: Heartbeat accepted. The `command` field is non-null when the backend has a pending instruction for the agent (e.g. JWT renewal). The agent must execute the command and may acknowledge it on the next cycle.

401.description: JWT invalid or node has been revoked. The agent must self-terminate and delete its local state file.

get.description: **Called by the EdgeHub agent** to retrieve its own node record from the control plane. Useful for agents to verify their registration status, check for configuration changes, or inspect their own metadata after startup.

200.description: The node record associated with the authenticated JWT.

401.description: JWT invalid or node has been revoked.

get.description: Returns every node across all sites. Each node includes a real-time snapshot of its latest telemetry (CPU, RAM, disk, uptime), current `status` (`online` / `failing` / `offline`), the `offline_cycles` counter, and any `pending_command` queued for the agent.

200.description: Flat array of all registered nodes.

get.description: Retrieves the full details of a single node by its UUID. Includes all mutable and immutable fields: hardware metadata registered at provisioning time, latest telemetry snapshot, status, JWT expiry, and any pending command.

patch.description: Partially updates the mutable fields of a node. Currently only `description` is updatable via this endpoint — hardware metadata (`hostname`, `os`, `arch`, `agent_type`) is set at registration time and is immutable.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: UUID of the node to update.

example.description: Primary edge node — updated rack location B2.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a node and **immediately invalidates its JWT**. On the next heartbeat attempt, the agent will receive `401 Unauthorized` and will self-terminate, deleting its local state file.

**Cascade behavior:** all heartbeat records associated with this node are also deleted.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: UUID of the node to delete.

204.description: Node deleted successfully. No content returned.

post.description:
Queues a JWT rotation command for the specified node. On the next heartbeat, the backend delivers an `update_jwt` command in the response, containing the new token. The agent replaces its stored JWT and begins using the new one immediately.

**Anti-spam protection:** if a renewal command is already pending (not yet picked up by the agent), subsequent calls to this endpoint are rejected until the pending command is consumed. This prevents token queue flooding.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: UUID of the node whose JWT should be rotated.

200.description: JWT renewal command queued successfully. The new token will be delivered to the agent on its next heartbeat.

message.example: JWT renewal queued. The agent will receive the new token on its next heartbeat.

409.description: A renewal command is already pending for this node. Wait for the agent to consume it before requesting another rotation.

get.description:
Returns the most recent N heartbeat records for a node, sorted from newest to oldest. Each record includes the full telemetry snapshot at that point in time, including any `extra_data` payload (Docker container states, Kubernetes pod health, etc.).

Use the `limit` query parameter to control how many records are returned (default: 100, no hard max enforced — use reasonable values for performance).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of heartbeat records to return, starting from the most recent.

200.description: Array of heartbeat records, newest first.

get.description: Returns the operational status of the EdgeHub API. Use this endpoint for liveness probes in container orchestrators (Docker Compose `healthcheck`, Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes, or uptime monitors).

admin_key.title: Admin API Key
admin_key.description: The secret Admin API Key configured in the EdgeHub backend environment variables.

SiteCreate.title: SiteCreate
SiteCreate.description: Payload to create a new site. Only `name` is required; Webhook URLs can be added at creation time or later via PATCH.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable name for the site (e.g. 'Production DC - Milan', 'Home Lab').

discord_webhook_url.title: Discord Webhook URL
discord_webhook_url.description: Discord Incoming Webhook URL. If set, the system will POST offline/recovery alert embeds to this endpoint.

slack_webhook_url.title: Slack Webhook URL
slack_webhook_url.description: Slack Incoming Webhook URL. If set, the system will POST offline/recovery alert messages to this endpoint.

SitePatch.title: SitePatch
SitePatch.description: Partial update schema for an existing site. All fields are optional. Pass `null` to a Webhook URL field to remove it.

description.title: Description
description.description: New description. Pass `null` to clear it.

discord_webhook_url.title: Discord Webhook URL
discord_webhook_url.description: Updated Discord Webhook URL. Pass `null` to disable Discord alerts for this site.

slack_webhook_url.title: Slack Webhook URL
slack_webhook_url.description: Updated Slack Webhook URL. Pass `null` to disable Slack alerts for this site.

id.title: Site ID
id.description: UUID assigned by the backend on creation.

TokenCreate.title: TokenCreate
TokenCreate.description: Configuration for a new registration token.

label.title: Label
label.description: Optional human-readable label to identify which node this token was intended for (e.g. 'prod-milan-node-01').

expires_in_hours.title: Expires In Hours
expires_in_hours.description: Token validity window in hours, starting from creation. Default is 24h; maximum is 8760h (1 year). After expiry, the token cannot be used even if not yet consumed.

TokenResponse.title: TokenResponse
TokenResponse.description: Full token details returned immediately after creation. The `raw_token` is shown **only once** — it is not stored in plaintext and cannot be retrieved again.

site_id.title: Site ID
site_id.description: UUID of the site this token is scoped to.

raw_token.title: Raw Token
raw_token.description: The plaintext registration token value. **Copy this now — it will not be shown again.**

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which the token is invalid.

commands.title: Deployment Commands
commands.description: Pre-built, copy-paste deployment commands keyed by agent mode (e.g. `linux`, `docker`, `kubernetes`). These are generated by the backend and include the token embedded in the correct environment variable.

TokenListItem.title: TokenListItem
TokenListItem.description: Token metadata returned in list responses. The raw token value is not included for security.

is_valid.title: Is Valid
is_valid.description: True if the token has not been used and has not expired. False if it was consumed by an agent or the expiry timestamp has passed.

AgentRegisterRequest.title: AgentRegisterRequest
AgentRegisterRequest.description: Registration payload sent by the agent on first startup. The `registration_token` is burned server-side upon successful validation.

registration_token.title: Registration Token
registration_token.description: The single-use provisioning token generated via `POST /api/v1/sites/{site_id}/tokens`.

hostname.title: Hostname
hostname.description: The node's hostname as reported by the OS.

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional human-readable description of the node (e.g. rack location, role).

agent_type.title: Agent Type
agent_type.description: Deployment mode of the agent. Must be one of: `linux` (native systemd daemon), `docker` (containerized), `kubernetes` (cluster deployment).

agent_version.title: Agent Version
agent_version.description: Semantic version of the EdgeHub Go agent binary (e.g. `1.2.0`).

os.title: Operating System
os.description: OS name and version string as reported by the agent (e.g. `Ubuntu 24.04 LTS`, `Alpine Linux 3.19`).

AgentRegisterResponse.title: AgentRegisterResponse
AgentRegisterResponse.description: Response returned after successful agent registration. The agent must persist both values in its local state file (`edgehub-state.json`).

node_id.title: Node ID
node_id.description: UUID of the newly created node record. Used to identify this node in the dashboard and API.

agent_token.title: Agent JWT
agent_token.description: Long-lived Bearer JWT to be used in the `Authorization` header for all subsequent agent-facing requests (heartbeat, `GET /agents/me`).

HeartbeatRequest.title: HeartbeatRequest
HeartbeatRequest.description: Telemetry snapshot sent by the agent on every heartbeat cycle. Core metrics are validated as percentages (0–100) or non-negative numbers. The `extra_data` field accepts any JSON object for environment-specific payloads.

cpu_usage.title: CPU Usage (%)
cpu_usage.description: Current CPU utilization as a percentage (0.0–100.0).

memory_usage.title: Memory Usage (%)
memory_usage.description: Current RAM utilization as a percentage (0.0–100.0).

disk_usage.title: Disk Usage (%)
disk_usage.description: Primary disk utilization as a percentage (0.0–100.0).

uptime_seconds.title: Uptime (seconds)
uptime_seconds.description: Node uptime in seconds since last boot.

ip_address.title: IP Address
ip_address.description: Primary IP address of the node as seen from its own network interface.

extra_data.title: Extra Data
extra_data.description: Arbitrary JSON payload for environment-specific telemetry. For Docker agents: container list with status/resource usage. For Kubernetes agents: pod and cluster health. Structure is agent-defined and stored in the hybrid JSON column — no schema migration required for new fields.

HeartbeatResponse.title: HeartbeatResponse
HeartbeatResponse.description: Backend acknowledgment of a heartbeat. The agent must inspect the `command` field on every response.

status.title: Status
status.description: Always `ok` when the heartbeat is accepted.

node_id.title: Node ID
node_id.description: UUID of the node that sent the heartbeat.

timestamp.title: Server Timestamp
timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp recorded by the server when the heartbeat was processed.

command.description: Optional command for the agent to execute. Currently used for `update_jwt` (JWT rotation). Null when no command is pending.

AgentCommand.title: AgentCommand
AgentCommand.description: An instruction delivered to the agent via the heartbeat response channel. The agent is responsible for executing the action and applying the payload.

action.title: Action
action.description: The command type. Currently supported: `update_jwt` — instructs the agent to replace its stored JWT with the value in `payload.new_token`.

NodeResponse.title: NodeResponse
NodeResponse.description: Full representation of a registered edge node, including its latest telemetry snapshot and operational state.

hostname.title: Hostname
hostname.description: Hostname as reported at registration time.

agent_version.title: Agent Version
agent_version.description: Version of the agent binary that registered this node.

cpu_usage.title: CPU Usage (%)
cpu_usage.description: CPU utilization from the last heartbeat.

mem_usage.title: Memory Usage (%)
mem_usage.description: RAM utilization from the last heartbeat.

status.title: Status
status.description: Current node status: `online` (receiving heartbeats), `failing` (1–2 missed cycles), `offline` (3+ missed cycles, alert sent).

last_seen.title: Last Seen
last_seen.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recently accepted heartbeat.

offline_cycles.title: Offline Cycles
offline_cycles.description: Number of consecutive missed heartbeat cycles. Resets to 0 on recovery. Alert is dispatched when this reaches 3.

offline_alert_sent.title: Offline Alert Sent
offline_alert_sent.description: True if the offline Webhook notification has been dispatched for the current outage period. Resets to false on recovery.

pending_command.title: Pending Command
pending_command.description: Command queued for the agent, to be delivered on its next heartbeat. Null when no command is pending.

jwt_expires_at.title: JWT Expires At
jwt_expires_at.description: ISO 8601 expiry timestamp of the node's current JWT. Use the `renew-jwt` endpoint to rotate before expiry.

NodePatch.title: NodePatch
NodePatch.description: Partial update schema for a node. Only user-defined metadata fields are mutable post-registration.

HeartbeatRecord.title: HeartbeatRecord
HeartbeatRecord.description: A single persisted telemetry snapshot from the node's heartbeat history. Returned by `GET /api/v1/nodes/{node_id}/heartbeats`.

id.title: Record ID
id.description: Auto-incremented integer primary key of the heartbeat record.

node_id.title: Node ID
node_id.description: UUID of the node this record belongs to.

extra_data.title: Extra Data
extra_data.description: Environment-specific telemetry payload stored as JSON (Docker container list, Kubernetes pod states, etc.).

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: ISO 8601 server-side timestamp when this heartbeat was recorded.

HTTPBearer.description: Agent JWT issued at registration via `POST /api/v1/agents/register`. Include in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for all agent-facing endpoints.
